description: Negative SHACL fixtures. Each file is crafted to violate exactly one shape. The gate asserts the expected result actually fires — matched on focus node, constraint component, path and severity, plus a message substring — not merely that the graph fails to conform. See README.md.
note: sourceShape is a BLANK NODE for every inline sh:property shape in music_vocabulary_shapes.ttl, so it cannot be matched by IRI. Match on the tuple below instead.

fixtures.why: A musician who plays nothing. Expected severity is Warning, not Violation — the shape carries node-level sh:severity sh:Warning. A gate keyed on Violation count would pass this while looking rigorous.

expect.message_contains: should play at least one instrument

fixtures.why: A song written by a record label.
An album produced by a record label. Labels release records; they do not produce them.
A composition composed by a record label.
A root genre claiming a parent. Would silently break the :hasGenre/:hasBroaderGenre* roll-up behind CQ-1 and CQ-9.

expect.message_contains: must not have a broader genre

fixtures.why: A peak chart position with no chart named is a meaningless number (CQ-10). Proves the SPARQL-constraint shapes fire, not just the property shapes.
An artist who retires eleven years before starting. Would corrupt CQ-13's era-overlap answers.

expect.message_contains: must not be later than activeUntil

fixtures.why: An untyped string where a gYear belongs — one of the style guide's five ruthless rules, and CQ-13 compares these years numerically.

expect.message_contains: activeFrom must be a single xsd:gYear
